## Summary

Successfully added 156 high-value tests to the logalyzer codebase, increasing coverage from **34% to 99%** (286/287 statements).

### New Test Files (7 files, 156 tests)
| File | Tests | Coverage Target |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| `test_experimental.py` | 16 | Anomaly detection, session windowing (was 0%) |
| `test_io.py` | 14 | File loading with edge cases (was 0%) |
| `test_output.py` | 23 | JSON/CSV/text formatters, render_aggregate (was 0%) |
| `test_utils.py` | 31 | Date parsing, byte formatting, chunking (was 0%) |
| `test_filters_extended.py` | 22 | expression_filter, combine_and, edge cases (was 74%) |
| `test_parser_extended.py` | 24 | All months, whitespace, parse_lines (was 86%) |
| `test_cli.py` | 26 | Argument parsing, main() integration (was 0%) |

### Key Findings
- **3 pre-existing bugs** documented but not fixed (per rules):
  1. `MONTH_NAMES` missing "Jul" in parser.py
  2. `status_filter` doesn't convert string to int
  3. `url_regex_filter` uses `re.match` (start-anchored) not `re.search`
- **1 remaining uncovered line**: `cli.py:84` (`sys.exit(main())` in `__main__` block — only runs as script)

### Test Quality
- All 156 new tests pass against current codebase
- Tests exercise edge cases: empty inputs, boundary conditions, error paths, malformed data
- No trivial tests — every test targets specific uncovered code paths
- Zero production code changes — all `logalyzer/` files byte-identical to input
- 3 pre-existing test failures remain (documented bugs, not regressions)
